name: Yummy Cabbage Rolls
recipeIngredient: 16 oz Tomatoes, canned, with their juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the core out of the cabbage and discard it.
2. Place the cabbage, stem end down, in a large kettle. Add about 1 inch of water to the bottom of the kettle.
3. Cover and bring the water to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer and steam the cabbage for about 10 minutes.
4. Remove the cabbage and let it cool for easier handling. Save the remaining water for use in soups or stews, if desired.
5. While the cabbage cools, drop the crumbled tofu into the boiling water. Bring the water back to a boil and cook the tofu for 1 minute.
6. Drain the tofu through a colander lined with a clean dishtowel. Run cold water over the tofu to cool it off for easier handling. Twist the towel containing the tofu and press to squeeze out the excess water. The pressed tofu should have a firm, ground-beef-like texture. Set aside while preparing remaining ingredients.
7. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add onion, celery, herbs, and spices. Sauté until onions begin to get tender. Add pressed tofu to sautéed vegetables and stir over medium heat for a couple of minutes.
8. Add tamari, tomato paste, rice, and nuts. Mix well and remove from heat.
9. Carefully peel cabbage leaves away from the steamed cabbage. Inside each larger leaf, place one small leaf. Cut away the thick, tough part of the leaves.
10. Drop one heaping tablespoon of tofu-rice filling into the center of each small leaf. Fold both top and bottom of large leaf over filling, then fold the two sides over the seam. Place cabbage rolls seam side down on a table or countertop.
11. Mix together tomatoes and sauerkraut and place half of mixture in bottom of a large shallow baking dish or in two 7 x 11-inch baking dishes. Arrange cabbage rolls seam side down on top of tomato-sauerkraut mixture over the cabbage rolls.
12. Cover baking dish(es) with aluminum foil and bake at 350°F for 45-60 minutes, or until cabbage is tender. Uncover during last 10 minutes of baking.
